meaning: n.暗示,略微的提示,略知,模糊概念
examples: "Digital textbooks are not going to catch on," says Inkling CEO Matt Maclnnis as he's giving me a demo over coffee.
He had no inkling of what was going on.
I had no inkling of his real purpose until much later.
If you've enjoyed Ethernets in big corporations, you have some inkling about always-on Internet access.
Baier had an inkling, mostly from the horror stories spreading through the klatch of VC-backed entrepreneurs.
